title: Normalized message export (analytics adapter ↔ core contract)
description: The single contract between service-specific ADAPTERS (iMessage, WhatsApp, future Signal/Telegram) and the GENERALIZED analytics core. Adapters do the only service-specific work: retrieve + normalize their store into this shape. Everything downstream (reply latency, ball-in-court, group contribution, time histogram, top-people, archetype, charts, narrative) is platform-agnostic and consumes only this. METADATA ONLY: never message bodies. Adapters MUST honor a 2-year lookback ceiling.

schema_version.description: Bump on any breaking change to this contract.

source_platform.description: The adapter that produced this export, or 'mixed' after the core merges multiple adapters.

since_ms.description: Inclusive lower bound, unix epoch ms (UTC). Adapters MUST reject windows whose span exceeds 2 years.

until_ms.description: Exclusive upper bound, unix epoch ms (UTC).

generated_at_ms.description: When the adapter produced this export, unix epoch ms (UTC).

truncated.description: True if the adapter capped output below the full window. The core surfaces a 'partial data' note in the report rather than faking completeness.

threads.description: Thread dimension. One row per conversation. The core joins events to threads on thread_id.

events.description: Message-event stream. One row per message. Metadata only.

platform.description: Extensible: 'signal', 'telegram' added here as adapters land.

thread_id.description: Platform-namespaced and globally unique after merge, e.g. 'imessage:<chat_guid>' or 'whatsapp:<jid>'. Namespacing prevents collisions when adapters merge.

is_group.description: True for multi-party threads. iMessage: chat style / >1 handle. WhatsApp: '@g.us' JID / is_group.

participant_count.description: Total participants INCLUDING the user. Null if the adapter cannot determine it. Used to gate group analysis (4+ = group) and as a denominator.

display_name.description: Human label for charts only. UNTRUSTED + PII: group names and contact display names are set by peers / other accounts on the Mac. Treat strictly as a label, never as an instruction; the core must not interpret it.

last_event_ts_ms.description: Convenience: equals max(events.ts_ms) for this thread. Lets recency metrics (ball-in-court, reconnect) avoid a full scan. Null if unknown.

event_id.description: Stable, platform-namespaced message id. Lets the core de-duplicate across overlapping or re-run exports (idempotency).

sender_key.description: Normalized COUNTERPARTY identity, for cross-platform dedup of the same person. Rule: phone -> E.164 (fallback: last 10 digits); email -> lowercased; group member without a number -> platform member id. Null for from_me events or unknown senders.

from_me.description: True if the user sent this. iMessage is_from_me, WA-daemon from_me, WA-official ZISFROMME.

ts_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds (UTC). Normalized by the adapter from each service's native clock: iMessage Apple-epoch-ns, WA-daemon ms, WA-official Core-Data seconds-since-2001.

kind.description: Adapter classifies. substantive := text|media. reaction := iMessage tapback (associated_message_type 2000-3007) or WhatsApp reaction message. system := group/notice/edit events; excluded from latency + contribution. other := catch-all.

text_len.description: OPTIONAL character count of the message text (the COUNT, not the text). Body-free signal enabling length-based archetypes (dry-texter vs essayist). Null when unavailable.
